behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_TANKARD::look' (examine) handler for the TANKARD object. When the player looks at the tankard it emits a single short description/bark text "tankard" and then the process suspends/ends.
There are no menu choices, flags read/written, parameters, or helper functions involved — the handler is minimal and purely descriptive.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TANKARD::look executes when the object is examined, sends a Bark/description 'tankard', then invokes suspend to pause/terminate the U8P_TANKARD::look process.
open_questions: What precise engine semantics does the called suspend perform here (yield, terminate the process, or wait for further input)?
Is there an implicit 'this' parameter provided to U8P_TANKARD::look by the engine (common for item processes) and is it used elsewhere in variations of this script?

main_function.name: U8P_TANKARD::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the player examining the TANKARD by displaying a short description ('tankard') and then suspending/ending the U8P_TANKARD::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_TANKARD::look/examine action on the TANKARD (processType 0x0208).
Inferred: player inspects or targets the tankard object.

function_map.name: U8P_TANKARD::look
function_map.role: object U8P_TANKARD::look/examine handler (emits a short description and ends the process)

external_calls.purpose: Pause or conclude the U8P_TANKARD::look process after emitting the description (inferred from raw 'suspend' node).
